sources.title: Creating custom packages
positive_evidence: Defines the supported custom-package workflow and conventional package layout
Requires package tests and recommends changelog maintenance for shared packages
limits: Does not define Inventory domain semantics
sources.license_boundary: Documentation may be summarized and linked; no source-code copy is required

sources.title: Adding tests to a package
positive_evidence: Defines Editor and Runtime test locations and test assembly requirements
limits: Does not prescribe domain test cases
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only

sources.title: Architect your code for efficient changes and debugging with ScriptableObjects
positive_evidence: Published by Unity from production guidance by Schell Games principal engineer Ryan Hipple
Advocates modular, data-driven systems without hard references between game systems
Uses Inventory as an example of replaceable state and recommends self-contained prefabs
limits: A pattern guide, not a complete Inventory implementation
sources.license_boundary: Conceptual reference; implementation is independently authored

sources.title: Nested Prefabs
positive_evidence: Defines supported nesting where child prefab instances retain links to their own assets
limits: Does not decide which Inventory UI components deserve prefab boundaries
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only

sources.title: Player inventory
positive_evidence: Separates configuration item IDs from unique player Inventory instance IDs
Models per-instance data, pagination, structured asynchronous operations, and optimistic write locks
limits: Service-specific limits and APIs are not imposed on the local core
Does not provide slot, grid, or offline presentation behavior
sources.license_boundary: Behavioral benchmark only; no SDK dependency in the core

sources.title: Lyra Inventory and Equipment
positive_evidence: Separates Inventory ownership and lifetime from Equipment presentation and lifetime
Separates shared item definitions from runtime item instances
Uses optional fragments to compose item capabilities without deep inheritance
limits: Unreal-specific replication, gameplay tags, actors, and abilities are not copied into the Unity API
sources.license_boundary: Cross-engine conceptual comparison only; no Unreal source is copied

sources.title: Ultimate Inventory System public documentation
positive_evidence: Documents definition, mutable instance, unique item, stack, collection, restriction, transaction, save, and UI-view concepts
Exposes extension seams for restrictions, import/export, save integration, and item views
limits: Commercial implementation is proprietary
Its singleton and category choices are benchmarked rather than adopted by default
sources.license_boundary: Public documentation informs coverage only; no proprietary code or assets are copied

sources.title: Inventory Engine documentation
positive_evidence: Separates invisible Inventory storage from InventoryDisplay
Documents multiple inventories, slots, equipment inventories, details, hotbars, actions, and save/load
Treats a display as composed grids, slots, and states rather than one indivisible panel
limits: Commercial implementation is proprietary
sources.license_boundary: Public documentation informs coverage only; no proprietary code or assets are copied

sources.title: Game Creator Inventory documentation
positive_evidence: Documents authored item assets, bags, grid shapes, inheritance, sockets, equipment, use actions, and crafting
Demonstrates designer-facing workflows for complex Inventory features
limits: Commercial implementation is proprietary
Instruction and condition frameworks are product-specific and remain optional integration seams
sources.license_boundary: Public documentation informs coverage only; no proprietary code or assets are copied

sources.title: XR Interaction Toolkit for Unity 6
positive_evidence: Defines the supported component framework for 3D and UI interaction in Unity 6
Current Unity 6 documentation lists released XRI versions and compatibility
limits: XR interaction belongs in an optional adapter, not the Inventory core
sources.license_boundary: Package API dependency is isolated to the XR adapter

sources.title: Project Validation
positive_evidence: Defines a first-party Unity project-configuration validation surface for PICO deployment
Classifies required, recommended, and optional configuration rules before a project is built
limits: Project validation does not prove installation, binocular readability, controller targeting, world anchoring, or comfort on a physical headset
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only; the public acceptance contract is independently authored

sources.title: Enable interaction between the controller ray and a canvas
positive_evidence: Requires the Canvas render mode to be World Space for the documented controller-ray UI path
Documents Tracked Device Graphic Raycaster setup and separate left/right XR Ray Interactor configuration
Requires ray reach to exceed the camera-to-UI distance
limits: Configuration instructions do not prove target isolation, readability, world anchoring, or comfort on a named device
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only; no sample source or images are copied

sources.title: Build and run the scene
positive_evidence: Defines Android as the target platform for the documented PICO XR build path
Separates producing the APK from selecting a connected PICO run device and launching the app on the headset
limits: A successful Build And Run does not establish Inventory UI quality or controller correctness
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only

sources.title: Controller and HMD input mapping
positive_evidence: Documents PICO controller and HMD actions as Unity XR input events
Provides model-specific controller mappings, supporting named-model and input-mode recording
limits: Input mapping does not prove a consumer scene routes a ray to the intended UI target
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only

sources.title: PICO Unity OpenXR SDK release notes
positive_evidence: Records required PICO device-system versions for Unity OpenXR SDK releases
Records device-runtime interaction limitations, demonstrating that an automated or visible ray does not by itself prove working device interaction
limits: Release notes do not define Inventory-specific target layout or acceptance thresholds
A pass remains scoped to the named device, firmware, runtime stack, APK, and package revision
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only; no SDK source or assets are copied

sources.title: World space UI
positive_evidence: Unity 6.3 documents world-space UI as a supported UI Toolkit route rather than a Canvas-only concern
Separates panel/rendering configuration from application presentation semantics
limits: World-space rendering documentation does not prove XR input, device readability, or comfort
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only; package code and assets are independently authored

sources.title: Create a world space UI
positive_evidence: Defines the Unity 6.3 UI Toolkit world-space document and panel composition path
Provides a renderer-specific basis for a UI Toolkit XR adapter without importing UGUI
limits: The example is not an Inventory system and does not establish a named-device result
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only; no example source or assets are copied

sources.title: Configure input for a world space panel
positive_evidence: Defines panel input as a distinct UI Toolkit configuration concern
Supports fail-closed validation that is independent of Canvas raycaster rules
limits: Panel configuration does not prove XR interactor targeting or headset usability
sources.license_boundary: Documentation reference only

sources.title: UI Toolkit support in XR Interaction Toolkit
positive_evidence: Documents supported ray and poke interaction with world-space UI Toolkit
Documents coexistence with UGUI while keeping their renderer configurations distinct
States the minimum Unity and XRI version boundary for this route
limits: Package support does not transfer evidence between renderers or devices
Known limitations and version-specific behavior must be rechecked at each pinned dependency revision
sources.license_boundary: Package API reference only; implementation remains inside a Unity-only adapter

sources.title: World Space UI sample
positive_evidence: Provides an official sample route for world-space UI Toolkit with XR interaction
Supports comparison of required panel, input, ray, and poke configuration
limits: Sample behavior is a configuration reference, not Inventory code or named-device acceptance evidence
sources.license_boundary: Reference only; sample source and assets are not copied

sources.title: A new 3D interaction paradigm for spatial applications
positive_evidence: Separates stable application actions from gaze, hand, controller, and poke interaction kinds
Supports keeping semantic Inventory intents independent of the active input modality
limits: Forward-looking PICO OS 6 guidance is not proof for the current Unity package, PICO firmware, or controller route
No support claim is made until the exact runtime and device profile is implemented and tested
sources.license_boundary: Conceptual public reference only

sources.title: Spatial layout
positive_evidence: Provides first-party spatial layout principles for legibility, placement, depth, and comfort considerations
limits: Design principles do not define PICO controller input or a Unity implementation
No Apple visual asset, proprietary material, or platform behavior is copied
sources.license_boundary: Principles are summarized and linked; no protected assets or source are copied

sources.title: Gestures
positive_evidence: Shows that gaze and pinch semantics require a platform-specific interaction adapter rather than tracked-controller assumptions
limits: The Vision Pro interaction model is not used as a substitute for PICO, Quest, or XRI device evidence
sources.license_boundary: Principles are summarized and linked; no protected assets or source are copied

sources.title: XR Interaction Toolkit Examples
positive_evidence: Official maintained sample with world-space UI prefabs, UI controls, presets, and interaction examples
Strong public adoption provides an additional operational signal
limits: Unity Companion License restricts use to Unity-dependent projects
Sample assets are not copied into the generic core
sources.license_boundary: Reference and Unity-only adapter comparison under the Unity Companion License
